action.reasoning: Observation: Iter10 crash from UNet size mismatch in ResBlock. Iter8 ResCNN mean_r2 = -7.4e7 despite val_loss~0.47, because low‑variance humidity dims cause huge negative R² even with small absolute error. Analysis: Replacing humidity predictions with the training‑set mean for near‑zero‑variance dimensions yields R²≈0 per dim, removing the massive negative penalty. Quick compute: if we set all 60 humidity dims to their mean, overall mean_r2 becomes (60×0.46 + 8×0.92)/128 ≈ 0.27, a huge jump. The ResCNN architecture works; we just need to fix the model.py and apply the post‑processing trick. Remaining time ~28 min; we can train a lighter ResCNN for 5 epochs (validate only at the end) and submit eval. Decision: 1) Remove buggy UNet from model.py, keep ClimateResCNN. 2) Update predict.py to load target_stats.npz and force humidity predictions to the training mean for dimensions with variance < 1e‑6. 3) Train for 5 epochs (smaller init_channels=64, 4 blocks) with weighted loss, validate at end, save checkpoint, submit eval.
